project.name: hypercore
project.description: A lean engineering system for Claude Code: sharp skills invoked on demand, a knowledge layer that maps the code, a session layer for parallel agents, and a thin external surface. Capability you reach for, not process you maintain.

nodes.name: .mcp.json
nodes.summary: MCP server configuration registering the Context7 server (launched via npx @upstash/context7-mcp) to provide agents with live, version-correct library documentation.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: Core agent operating rules for hypercore: defines the five layers, the always-on behavioral floor, task routing, the Align to Compound loop, and the full skill index. Loaded every session as the behavioral floor.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Claude Code project rules entry point; mirrors AGENTS.md (symlinked) so the same hypercore operating rules — five layers, behavioral floor, routing, loop, and skill index — load automatically every session.

nodes.name: CONCEPTS.md
nodes.summary: Durable domain vocabulary registry: a glossary of project-specific terms appended by the ce-compound skill as concepts stabilize, so agents speak the same language across sessions.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project overview and setup guide for hypercore: maps the five layers, summarizes the engineering loop, and walks through plugin install, Context7, the knowledge layer, and NTM parallel-session setup.

nodes.name: SKILLS-ATTRIBUTION.md
nodes.summary: Provenance and licensing record for hypercore's skill set: tables of vendored MIT skills with sources and authors plus the three in-house authored skills, documenting per-skill modifications.

nodes.name: marketplace.json
nodes.summary: Claude Code plugin marketplace manifest declaring the hypercore plugin, its local source (./), owner, and version for distribution.

nodes.name: plugin.json
nodes.summary: Plugin manifest for hypercore describing its name, description, version, author, license, and keywords for the Claude Code plugin system.

nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: Ignore-pattern file (gitignore syntax) controlling which files and directories the Understand-Anything analysis excludes; ships with commented suggestions to uncomment.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses .gitignore glob syntax with # comments and ! negation; all patterns are commented out by default.

nodes.name: hypercore-activate.sh
nodes.summary: One-shot idempotent activation script that wires the NTM project base, installs the destructive-git block hook, enables yolo permissions for Claude Code and Codex, and commits/pushes the hypercore layer of both repos.
nodes.languageNotes: Backs up every edited config to *.hypercore.bak and installs the safety rail before enabling yolo so agents never get prompt-free git destruction.

nodes.name: install.sh
nodes.summary: Preflight checker that inspects the external tool surface (rg, fd, eza, gh, tmux, ntm, Context7, skills) and prints exact, non-destructive setup instructions for anything missing.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Explains the docs/solutions corpus — one Markdown file per solved problem written by ce-compound — and its role as the payload a later research or planning pass reads back in.

nodes.name: ce-compound/SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Skill that captures a just-solved problem as a durable solution doc under docs/solutions/ and records project-specific domain vocabulary in CONCEPTS.md so knowledge compounds instead of being relearned.

nodes.name: code-review/SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Two-axis code review skill that diffs HEAD against a fixed point and runs parallel sub-agents for Standards (repo coding conventions) and Spec (originating issue/PRD) review, reporting the results side by side.

nodes.name: hitl-loop.template.sh
nodes.summary: Copy-and-edit shell template for a human-in-the-loop reproduction loop; provides step and capture helpers so the agent runs the script while the user follows terminal prompts, emitting KEY=VALUE results for the agent to parse.

nodes.name: diagnosing-bugs/SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Six-phase diagnosis loop for hard bugs and performance regressions — build a feedback loop, reproduce and minimise, hypothesise, instrument, fix with a regression test, then clean up and post-mortem.

nodes.name: executing-plans/SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Skill for executing a written implementation plan task-by-task with review checkpoints, stopping to ask for help on blockers and never touching the main branch without consent.

nodes.name: finishing-a-development-branch/SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Skill guiding branch completion after tests pass — verify tests, detect environment, determine base branch, then present structured options to merge locally, push and open a PR, keep as-is, or discard, and finally clean up the workspace.

nodes.name: block-dangerous-git.sh
nodes.summary: Claude Code PreToolUse(Bash) hook that reads the proposed command and blocks destructive git operations — force-push, reset --hard, clean -f, branch -D, checkout/restore ., and history rewrites — while allowing normal git push.
nodes.languageNotes: Reads tool input JSON via jq and exit-2 blocks on any DANGEROUS_PATTERNS regex match; adapted from mattpocock/skills (MIT), narrowed to force-pushes only.

nodes.name: git-guardrails-claude-code/SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Skill that installs the Claude Code hook blocking dangerous git commands before execution — walking through scope, copying the hook script, wiring it into settings, offering customization, and verifying.

nodes.name: grill-me/SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Thin user-invocable entry point (disable-model-invocation) that triggers a grilling session to relentlessly stress-test a plan or design.

nodes.name: grilling/SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Skill that interviews the user one question at a time to stress-test a plan, walking the design tree and resolving decisions before any building begins.

nodes.name: handoff/SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Skill that compacts the current conversation into a redacted handoff document (with a suggested-skills section) saved to the OS temp directory so a fresh agent can continue the work.

nodes.name: receiving-code-review/SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Skill for weighing code-review feedback with technical rigor rather than performative agreement — verify before implementing, push back when warranted, and acknowledge correct feedback.

nodes.name: research/SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Skill that delegates reading legwork to a background agent, verifies each claim adversarially against primary sources and Context7, and returns one cited, confidence-tagged Markdown file under research/.

nodes.name: uat-signoff/SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Human acceptance sign-off skill that walks the owner through one observable test at a time to confirm a user-facing or high-risk change is what they wanted, inferring severity for any gaps from their own words.

nodes.name: verification-before-completion/SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Non-skippable gate skill requiring that any done/fixed/passing claim be backed by running the real verification command and reading its full output — evidence before assertions, always.

nodes.name: writing-plans/plan-document-reviewer-prompt.md
nodes.summary: Prompt template for dispatching a subagent that reviews a written plan for completeness, spec alignment, task decomposition, and buildability before implementation begins.

nodes.name: writing-plans/SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Skill for turning a spec into an executable, right-sized, bite-sized task plan — with a proper document header, no placeholders, and a self-review pass before handing off to execution.

nodes.name: writing-skills/SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Authoring discipline for hypercore's own skills — fuses structural best-practices (progressive disclosure, matched degrees of freedom, positive prompting) with a RED/GREEN/REFACTOR eval discipline that proves each skill against a baseline before it ships.

layers.name: Operating Rules
layers.description: The behavioral floor, task-routing loop, and durable vocabulary that every hypercore session loads: AGENTS.md, its CLAUDE.md symlink mirror, and the CONCEPTS glossary.

layers.name: Skill Catalog (Actions)
layers.description: The invocable skill catalog: fifteen SKILL.md capabilities (grilling, writing-plans, executing-plans, code-review, verification, handoff, research, and more) that agents reach for by name.

layers.name: Skill Helper Scripts & Templates
layers.description: Executable helpers and prompt/loop templates bundled inside individual skills: the git-guardrails PreToolUse hook, the HITL bug-reproduction template, and the plan-document reviewer prompt.

layers.name: Setup & Activation Scripts
layers.description: Shell scripts that bootstrap and activate the system: install.sh preflight external-tool checks and hypercore-activate.sh (NTM base, git-hook wiring, permissions, commit/push).

layers.name: Distribution & External-Tool Config
layers.description: Distribution and tool-wiring config: the Claude plugin and marketplace manifests, the Context7 MCP server registration, and the understand-anything ignore-pattern file.

layers.name: Knowledge & Documentation
layers.description: The project narrative and compounding knowledge surface: the README overview/setup guide, the skill provenance/licensing attribution, and the docs/solutions corpus README.

tour.title: Start Here — The Map
tour.description: hypercore isn't an application; it's an operating system for an AI engineer. The README is the map: it names the five layers (Rules, Actions, Knowledge, Sessions, Externals) and the setup path. Read it first to get the shape of the whole system before diving into any one part.

tour.title: The Operating Rules & the Five Layers
tour.description: AGENTS.md is the behavioral floor loaded every session (CLAUDE.md is a symlink mirror so Claude Code picks it up automatically). It defines the five layers, the always-on rules, task routing (the 'trivial vs. everything else' escape hatch), and the full skill index. This is the constitution the whole system runs under.

tour.title: Align — Pin Down Intent Before Building
tour.description: The loop opens with alignment. `grilling` interviews you one question at a time to stress-test a plan and resolve open decisions; `grill-me` is the thin user-invocable trigger for that session. Nothing gets built until intent is sharp.

tour.title: Plan — Turn Intent into an Executable Plan
tour.description: `writing-plans` converts agreed intent into a right-sized, bite-sized task plan with a proper header and a self-review pass. Its companion prompt template dispatches a reviewer sub-agent to check the plan for completeness and buildability before any code is written.

tour.title: Build — Execute the Plan, Diagnose When Stuck
tour.description: `executing-plans` works the plan task-by-task with review checkpoints, stopping on blockers and never touching main without consent. When a bug bites, `diagnosing-bugs` runs a six-phase loop; its HITL shell template lets the agent drive a reproduction while you follow terminal prompts.

tour.title: Verify & Sign-off — The One Invariant
tour.description: `verification-before-completion` is the gate that is never skipped: no 'done/fixed/passing' claim without running the real command and reading its full output. For user-facing, irreversible, or ambiguous changes, `uat-signoff` then walks the human owner through observable tests one at a time.

tour.title: Review — Produce It, Then Weigh It
tour.description: `code-review` runs the diff across two axes — Standards (repo conventions) and Intent (the originating spec) — via parallel sub-agents. `receiving-code-review` is the counterweight: verify feedback with technical rigor and push back when warranted instead of rubber-stamping it.

tour.title: Land & Compound — Don't Relearn What You Solved
tour.description: `finishing-a-development-branch` lands finished work (verify → merge/PR/cleanup). Then `ce-compound` closes the loop by capturing the solved problem into docs/solutions/ and durable vocabulary into CONCEPTS.md, so the next session starts ahead instead of from scratch.

tour.title: The Always-On Safety Rail & Support Skills
tour.description: Not everything is an invoked step. `git-guardrails-claude-code` installs a PreToolUse hook (block-dangerous-git.sh) that blocks force-push, reset --hard, clean -fd, and history rewrites before they run. Alongside the loop sit support skills reachable any time: `research` (delegated cited investigation), `handoff` (compact state for a fresh agent), and `writing-skills` (author/edit skills, eval-first).

tour.title: Setup, Distribution & External Surface
tour.description: Finally, how hypercore installs and reaches the outside world. `bin/install.sh` preflights the external tool surface; `bin/hypercore-activate.sh` wires the git hook, permissions, and NTM base. The plugin manifests distribute it as a Claude Code plugin, `.mcp.json` registers Context7 for live library docs, `.understandignore` scopes this knowledge graph, and SKILLS-ATTRIBUTION.md records the MIT provenance of every skill.
